data.title: EverQuote CEO Jayme Mendal Sells 14,360 Shares
data.description:
Reporter NameMendal Jayme
RelationshipCEO and President
TypeSell
Amount$328,269
SEC FilingForm 4

EverQuote CEO and President, Jayme Mendal, sold 14,360 shares of Class A Common Stock on August 20, 2025, for a total sale amount of $328,269. The weighted average selling price per share was $22.86, with sales conducted at prices ranging from $22.68 to $23.02. Following the transaction, Mendal directly owns 512,780 shares of EverQuote. The sale was executed under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan adopted on December 17, 2024.

SEC Filing: EverQuote, Inc. [ EVER ] - Form 4 - Aug. 22, 2025

data.title: Communications Services Up, but Not by Much, on Rate Rotation — Communications Services Roundup
data.description:
Communications-services companies ticked up, but not by as much as the broad market, as traders rotated into more rate-sensitive sectors.

Disney's ESPN is in advanced talks for a three-year deal with Major League Baseball worth around $550 million per year that would incorporate the MLB.tv service into the sports network's streaming service.

Earlier this week, similar reports indicated that Comcast's NBCUniversal was in negotiations with MLB to carry games on NBC and the Peacock streaming service in a three-year pact approaching $200 million annually. Streaming services are jockeying for position in live sports, which is seen as one of the last content categories that consumers are willing to pay for via premium, long-term subscriptions.

TikTok plans to lay off hundreds of workers in the U.K., as the video platform turns to artificial intelligence to automate content moderation.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Utilities Up After Powell's Jackson Hole Speech — Utilities Roundup
data.description:
Shares of power producers rose alongside Treasury bonds in the wake of Chairman Jerome Powell's speech at Jackson Hole.

"Chairman Powell's comments at Jackson Hole were more dovish than we and markets were expecting," said strategists at brokerage Bank of America Global Research, in a note to clients. "Powell was clearly spooked by the downward revisions to payrolls, as there haven't been other major dovish developments since his hawkish July presser."

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: LB Pharmaceuticals Seeks Nasdaq Global Market Listing Under Symbol LBRX
FutureTech II Acquisition Corp. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
FutureTech II Acquisition Corp., a blank check company formed to effectuate a merger, capital stock exchange, asset acquisition, stock purchase, reorganization, or similar business combination with one or more businesses, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report provides a detailed overview of the company's financial performance and key business activities during the period.

Financial Highlights

Net Income:

  • For the three months ended June 30, 2025, the company reported net losses of $80,647, which consisted of investment income of $72,477, partially offset by expenses of $138,735 and tax expense of $14,389.
  • For the six months ended June 30, 2025, the company reported net losses of $370,320, which consisted of investment income of $272,304, partially offset by expenses of $592,572 and tax expense of $50,052.

Net Income Per Share:

  • For the three months ended June 30, 2025, basic and diluted net income (loss) per share of common stock was $(0.19) for redeemable shares and $(0.04) for non-redeemable shares.
  • For the six months ended June 30, 2025, basic and diluted net income (loss) per share of common stock was $(0.04) for redeemable shares and $(0.15) for non-redeemable shares.

Liquidity and Capital Resources:

  • As of June 30, 2025, the company had $160,723 in cash and no cash equivalents, with cash used in operating activities amounting to $1,187,204 for the six months ended June 30, 2025.

Cash Provided by Investing Activities:

  • For the six months ended June 30, 2025, cash provided by investing activities was $17,585,764 due to cash withdrawn from the Trust Account of $17,822,230, partially offset by cash deposited into the Trust Account of $236,466.

Cash Used in Financing Activities:

  • For the six months ended June 30, 2025, cash used in financing activities was $16,294,605 due to $17,400,674 in cash paid for redemptions, partially offset by proceeds from Transfer Agent of $695,024 and proceeds from issuance of debt – related party of $411,045.

Business Highlights

Business Overview: FutureTech II Acquisition Corp is a blank check company formed to effectuate a merger, capital stock exchange, asset acquisition, stock purchase, reorganization, or similar business combination with one or more businesses. The company is an emerging growth company and is subject to the associated risks.

Extension of Combination Period: The company has extended its business combination period multiple times, with the latest extension allowing it to consummate its initial business combination by August 18, 2025. This extension is facilitated by monthly deposits into the trust account.

Merger Agreement with Longevity Biomedical Inc.: FutureTech II has entered into a merger agreement with Longevity Biomedical Inc., which involves Longevity merging with a subsidiary of FutureTech II, becoming a wholly-owned subsidiary. The company's common stock is expected to list on NASDAQ under the ticker symbol 'LBIO' post-merger.

PIPE Subscription Agreement: The company entered into a subscription agreement with an investor for the purchase of 1,000,000 shares of Class A Common Stock at $5.00 per share, contingent upon the consummation of the business combination with Longevity.

Sponsor Working Capital Loans: To finance transaction costs related to the Longevity Business Combination and ongoing operations, the company issued a promissory note to the sponsor for up to $1,500,000.

Compliance with Nasdaq Listing Standards: The company faced challenges in meeting Nasdaq's listing standards, resulting in a transfer from the Nasdaq Global Market to the Nasdaq Capital Market. The company is now trading over the counter following a delisting notice due to not completing a business combination within the required timeframe.

Zero Interest Convertible Notes: The company issued zero interest convertible notes totaling $1,025,000 to several investors, with the option to convert the principal amount into shares of the company post-business combination.

Future Outlook: The company is focused on completing its business combination with Longevity Biomedical Inc. and has extended its business combination period to ensure sufficient time for the transaction. The company is also working on regaining compliance with listing standards and expects its securities to be quoted on the over-the-counter market.

SEC Filing: FutureTech II Acquisition Corp. [ FTII ] - 10-Q - Aug. 22, 2025

data.title: Paramount preparing to cut as much as 3,000 jobs by early November, Variety reports
data.description:
Paramount is looking to cut between 2,000 to 3,000 jobs by early November, after the completion of its merger with production studio Skydance, Variety reported on Friday.

Paramount Global and Skydance Media completed their $8.4 billion merger — announced more than a year ago — earlier this month and the combined company was renamed Paramount Skydance Corp .

The layoffs will affect staff across divisions and the numbers could vary, the Variety report said.

As of December 2024, Paramount had nearly 18,600 full- and part-time employees, and 3,500 project-based staff.

Paramount Skydance did not immediately respond to a Reuters request for comment on the report.

On Monday, just days after finalizing its merger with production studio Skydance, Paramount said it will pay $7.7 billion for exclusive U.S. broadcast rights to the Ultimate Fighting Championship for seven years — the first major strategic move by the combined company.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Markets Headlines at 5 PM ET: Powell Highlights Job Market Worries, Opening Path to Rate Cut | Dow ...
data.description:
Powell Highlights Job Market Worries, Opening Path to Rate Cut

The Fed chair opened the door for rate cuts next month when he said the labor market might be softening enough to rein in inflation that is being pushed up by tariffs.

----

Dow Hits Record as Fed Chair Opens Door to Rate Cut

U.S. stocks surged, boosting the Dow to its first record close this year after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell opened the door for the central bank to cut rates next month.

----

Canada to Remove 25% Tariff on Some U.S. Goods

The move is seen as a sign of goodwill from Ottawa as it tries to reset its trading relationship with the U.S.

----

Europe's Fragile Energy Lifeline Exposed by Ukraine's Strikes in Russia

Ukraine's attacks, encouraged by President Trump, have disrupted flows and triggered angry reactions from regional leaders.

----

Canadians Tap the Brakes After Hitting the Stores in June

Indications of a pullback in retail spending by Canadians last month following a rebound in June continues a stretch of choppy trade and could signal that household consumption is cooling amid the stresses of the trade conflict with the U.S.

----

Eurozone Wage Growth Gains Pace, Likely Adding to ECB Caution

Wages in the eurozone rose at a slightly faster pace during the three months through June as unemployment remained at record lows.

----

U.K. Consumers Sunnier as BOE Cuts Rates But Economic Fears Remain

U.K. consumers felt a little better about their finances this month as the Bank of England lowered borrowing costs, though sentiment remains weak amid wider economic turmoil.

----

Japan's Inflation Stays Well Above BOJ's 2% Target

Japan's consumer inflation cooled in July, but remained well above the central bank's target of 2%, reinforcing expectations of a coming interest-rate increase.

----

The Biggest Retailers Are Thriving in the Tariff Economy

Walmart, Amazon and T.J. Maxx are beating out rivals by offering deals to stressed-out consumers.

----

Can Germany's Merz Revive the Economy? Voters Are Running Out of Patience

Polls show voters are growing restless with Friedrich Merz, raising pressure on the chancellor to deliver on the domestic stage.

----

Why Solar and Wind Power Can Thrive Without Subsidies

Rising electricity demand, in part due to AI needs, along with the increasing cost of alternatives should cushion the impact for green energy.

----

How Trump Will Decide Which Chips Act Companies Must Give Up Equity

The administration isn't eyeing equity in semiconductor companies that are increasing U.S. investment like TSMC and Micron, but may take stakes in others.

----

Peter Thiel Leads Pack of Investors Piling Into Ether

Proponents see Ethereum becoming the platform of choice for the financial world.

The move comes after days of discussion in Washington about options for the troubled American chip-maker's future.

data.title: 12 Cheap Consumer Stocks for a Rocky Market — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Ian Salisbury

With the market looking frothy, investors may want to check out stocks that tend to hold up in a selloff. Consumer staples shares, many of which boast attractive yields and valuations, could fit the bill.

Morningstar compiled a list of a dozen names in the sector that its analysts consider undervalued. "These companies provide goods and services that consumers will purchase and use regardless of whether times are good or bad," wrote Tori Brovet and Margaret Giles in a report Thursday.

The picks have a lot going for them. Most names will be familiar to investors and consumers alike, such as Campbell's, Kraft Heinz, and Clorox. All three have brands that have proven they can weather all kinds of economic climates.

What is more, almost all of the 12 stocks are trading at below the S&P 500's average forward price-to-earnings ratio of 22. A number of them boast attractive dividend yields, such as General Mills at 4.9% and Clorox at 4.1%.

The picks do involve risks, however. With the market on a hot streak, slow-and-steady consumer stocks haven't been much in fashion. While the S&P 500 has returned about 11% so far in 2025, the Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR is up just 6.3%.

Many of these companies' troubles go beyond investors' preference for hot tech shares. They have struggled with tariffs, gloomy consumer sentiment, and penny pinching by shoppers burned by inflation. General Mills, Campbell's, and Constellation Brands are down by double-digit percentages so far in 2025.

Of course, those share price declines are an important reason the stocks showed as undervalued in Morningstar's screen. Brovet and Giles say they looked for companies with strong business "moats" — the capacity to fend off competitors — and big price discounts to Morningstar's fair value estimates.

Morningstar's 12 Undervalued Consumer Defensive Stocks

data.title: QcX Gold Announces Private Placement
data.description:
Toronto, Ontario--(Newsfile Corp. - August 22, 2025) - QcX Gold Corp. (OTC Pink: QCXGF) (FSE: 21MA) ("QcX" or the "Company") is pleased to announce a non-brokered private placement financing for gross proceeds of up to $750,000 through the issuance of up to 7,500,000 units (the "Units") at a price of $0.10 per Unit (the "Offering").

Each Unit is comprised of one common share of the Company (each, a "Common Share") and one whole Common Share purchase warrant (each, a "Warrant") of the Company. Each Warrant entitling the holder thereof to purchase one Common Share at a price of $0.15 per Common Share for a period of twenty-four (24) months from the date of issuance.

The Company intends to use the net proceeds from the Offering for continued exploration and evaluation of the Company's Golden Giant and Fernet properties and for general working capital. Closing of the Offering is subject to receipt of all necessary corporate and regulatory approvals, including the approval of TSX Venture Exchange. All securities issued in connection with the Offering will be subject to a hold period of four months plus a day from the date of issuance and the resale rules of applicable securities legislation.

This press release does not constitute an offer to sell or a solicitation of an offer to buy the securities in the United States. The securities have not been and will not be registered under the United States Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the "U.S. Securities Act") or any state securities laws and may not be offered or sold within the United States or to U.S. Persons as defined under applicable United States securities laws unless registered under the U.S. Securities Act and applicable state securities laws or an exemption from such registration is available.

About QcX Gold

QcX Gold is exploring for gold and VMS style mineralization on its highly prospective and well-located properties in Québec, Canada. The Golden Giant Project is located in the James Bay region, only 2.9 km from Azimut Exploration Inc.'s Patwon discovery on their Elmer gold project. The Fernet Project is located in the Abitibi Greenstone Belt and is contiguous with Wallbridge Mining Company Limited's Fenelon/Martinière property. Both properties are in close proximity to major discoveries which bodes well for exploration.

On behalf of the Board of Directors:

Aaron Stone, P.Geo.

Vice President Explorationaaron.stone@qcxgold.com

416-361-2515

Neither the TSX Venture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

Forward-looking statements:

This news release contains forward-looking statements. All statements, other than of historical facts, that address activities, events or developments that the Company believes, expects or anticipates will or may occur in the future including, without limitation, the planned exploration program, the expected positive exploration results, the timing of the exploration results, the ability of the Company to continue with the exploration program, the availability of the required funds to continue with the exploration and the potential mineralization or potential mineral resources are forward-looking statements. Forward-looking statements are generally identifiable by use of the words "will", "should", "continue", "expect", "anticipate", "estimate", "believe", "intend", "to earn", "to have', "plan" or "project" or the negative of these words or other variations on these words or comparable terminology. Forward-looking statements are subject to a number of risks and uncertainties, many of which are beyond the Company's ability to control or predict, that may cause the actual results of the Company to differ materially from those discussed in the forward-looking statements. Factors that could cause actual results or events to differ materially from current expectations include, among other things, failure to meet expected, estimated or planned exploration expenditures, failure to establish estimated mineral resources, the possibility that future exploration results will not be consistent with the Company's expectations, general business and economic conditions, changes in world gold markets, sufficient labour and equipment being available, changes in laws and permitting requirements, unanticipated weather changes, title disputes and claims, environmental risks as well as those risks identified in the Company's annual Management's Discussion and Analysis. Should one or more of these risks or uncertainties materialize, or should assumptions underlying the forward-looking statements prove incorrect, actual results may vary materially from those described and accordingly, readers should not place undue reliance on forward-looking statements. Although the Company has attempted to identify important risks, uncertainties and factors which could cause actual results to differ materially, there may be others that cause results not to be as anticipated, estimated or intended. The Company does not intend, and does not assume any obligation, to update these forward-looking statements except as otherwise required by applicable law.

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/263549

data.title: Rocket Lab shares rise after announcing US investments expansion
data.description:
** Shares of space company Rocket Lab rise 1.6% to $45.09 in extended trading

** RKLB says it will increase U.S. investments to boost production of space-grade compound semiconductors, solar cells and electro-optical sensors

** Company says the plan, with its $275 million acquisition of electro‑optical payload provider Geost, will roughly double monthly wafer capacity to nearly 35,000 from 20,000 per month

** The investments will expand company's U.S.-based headcount to more than 2,000

** As of Friday's close, stock up ~74% YTD

Financials Up, as Fed Pivot Seen Buoying Banks, Deals, Markets — Financials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of banks and other financial institutions rose, after Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell's pivot to a rate-cutting stance.

For banks, the cut could stir demand for mortgages and other loans while the commitment to some elevation in rates would keep profit margins steady. Rate cuts are also likely to stir buying activity in bond, stock and deal markets.

Among dealmakers, Goldman Sachs Group shares rose 3.6%, testing all-time highs.

Intuit shares fell after the owner of tax-preparation software TurboTax forecast earnings growth short of some investors' expectations.

JPMorgan Chase will pay $330 million to settle claims that it facilitated transactions in the looting of a Malaysian sovereign-wealth fund, one of the largest financial crimes of the century.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Madison Technologies Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Madison Technologies Inc., a company focused on launching BlockchainTV (BCTV), a 24/7 television broadcast and streaming network dedicated to cryptocurrency information and entertainment, has released its Form 10-K report for the year ended December 31, 2023. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the evolving cryptocurrency media landscape.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues: $0. The company did not generate any revenues from continuing operations for the year ended December 31, 2023.
  • Net loss from continuing operations: $5.29 million. The decrease in net loss was primarily due to reductions in professional fees, general and administrative expenses, and interest expense.
  • Net loss from discontinued operations: $9,709. The loss from discontinued operations was significantly reduced due to the transfer of Sovryn's assets.
  • Net loss: $5.30 million. The overall net loss decreased from the previous year, reflecting cost-cutting measures and the winding down of certain operations.
  • Loss from continuing operations per share, basic and diluted: $0.0033. Reflects the impact of reduced operating expenses and interest costs.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: Madison Technologies Inc. is focused on creating and launching BlockchainTV (BCTV), a 24/7 television broadcast and streaming network dedicated to cryptocurrency information and entertainment. The company aims to fill an information void in the blockchain community by providing credible and unbiased content.
  • Product and Services: BCTV plans to deliver content through strategic partnerships and distribution deals across North America, utilizing platforms such as Roku, Hulu, YouTube, Pluto, and Xumo. The core revenue streams are expected to come from advertising, sponsorships, and e-commerce transactions.
  • Recent Developments: The company experienced a change of control in 2023, with Thomas Amon appointed as the sole member of the Board of Directors and assuming multiple executive roles. This change followed financial difficulties and defaults on debt obligations.
  • Discontinued Operations: In early 2023, Madison Technologies transferred ownership of its FCC licenses and broadcast television assets to a third-party entity controlled by investors, resulting in a reduction of indebtedness and the classification of these operations as discontinued.
  • Competitive Conditions: BCTV intends to compete in a fragmented and niche market, with major media organizations like Bloomberg and Comcast providing cryptocurrency content but lacking a dedicated source for continuous consumption.
  • Dependence on Customers: The business model is designed to generate revenue from a diverse customer base, including advertisers, sponsors, viewers, and third-party networks leasing channels on the OTA Platform.
  • Technology and Intellectual Property: The company currently does not own any patents, trademarks, or other intellectual property.
  • Governmental and Industry Regulations: Madison Technologies may be subject to FCC regulations if it continues its business operations as planned. The company must obtain FCC approval for various broadcasting activities.
  • Facilities: The principal executive office is located in Purchase, New York, where minimal operations are conducted.
  • Employee Structure: The company has one employee who serves in multiple executive roles, including President, CEO, and CFO.
  • Future Outlook: Following the change of control, the company has minimal operations and nominal assets. Discussions are ongoing to continue the business plans for BCTV, but there is no guarantee of success or timing.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Restructuring: The company undertook significant restructuring by transferring ownership of Sovryn's broadcast television business to a lender, which resulted in a $9,159,907 reduction in senior secured notes. This move was part of a broader strategy to focus on core business activities and reduce debt obligations.
  • Capital Management: The company faced a working capital deficit of $17,585,746 as of December 31, 2023, compared to $12,372,123 in the previous year. The increase in the deficit was primarily due to the transfer of Sovryn assets. The company raised $363,710 through subordinated loans and borrowings from finance companies to support its operations. Additionally, the company issued promissory notes and convertible notes with embedded conversion features and warrants, which were accounted for as equity instruments.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates requiring additional capital to meet both short-term and long-term operating requirements. It plans to raise additional funds through the sale of equity or debt securities. The company also intends to continue focusing on its core business, BCTV, and explore opportunities to renegotiate or convert existing notes payable into common stock to improve its financial position.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market Acceptance: The company faces significant challenges in achieving market acceptance for its products and services, as it has not yet begun offering them for sale. The competitive landscape includes major media organizations that provide cryptocurrency content, though none are dedicated solely to it.
  • Financial Risks: The company faces numerous risks, including a history of losses and uncertainty in achieving profitability. It has incurred significant debt, some of which is in default, potentially restricting operational flexibility.
  • Operational Risks: The company’s products may not achieve market acceptance due to competitive pressures, unfavorable publicity, and regulatory timing. Additionally, the company is dependent on acquiring new customers in competitive markets, which may be challenging due to the presence of well-resourced competitors.
  • Infrastructure and Personnel: Operational risks include the need for reliable performance of its broadcast assets and infrastructure, which are critical for maintaining its brand and reputation. The company is also dependent on key personnel, particularly Thomas Amon, and faces challenges in attracting and retaining qualified employees.
  • Cybersecurity Threats: Cybersecurity threats pose a significant risk, with potential breaches leading to reputational damage and financial liabilities.
  • Regulatory Risks: Regulatory risks are substantial, with the company potentially subject to various domestic and international laws related to privacy, data protection, and information security. Compliance with these regulations could result in significant costs and liabilities.
  • Liquidity and Capital Needs: The company’s liquidity position is precarious, with a significant working capital deficit and no cash on hand as of December 31, 2023. Management acknowledges the need for additional capital to meet long-term operating requirements and plans to raise funds through equity or debt securities.
  • Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in economic conditions that could impact revenue and operating results. The volatility in financial markets, driven by factors such as inflation, geopolitical tensions, and global supply chain disruptions, could adversely affect the company’s business. Additionally, the company’s stock is subject to significant price volatility, which could lead to securities class action litigation and impact its ability to raise capital.

SEC Filing: Madison Technologies Inc. [ MDEX ] - 10-K - Aug. 22, 2025

data.title: Kimball Electronics, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Kimball Electronics, Inc., a global provider of electronics manufacturing services, has released its Form 10-K report for the fiscal year 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in a competitive industry. The company operates in three primary end markets: automotive, medical, and industrial, with a significant global footprint that includes manufacturing facilities in the United States, China, Mexico, Poland, Romania, and Thailand.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Sales: $1,486.7 million, decreased by 13% compared to fiscal year 2024, primarily due to declines in automotive, medical, and industrial markets.
  • Gross Profit: $104.4 million, representing 7.0% of Net Sales, down from 8.2% in fiscal year 2024, due to lost absorption on lower revenue.
  • Operating Income: $45.5 million, decreased by 8% from fiscal year 2024, reflecting restructuring expenses and cost reduction efforts.
  • Net Income: $17.0 million, a decrease of 17% from fiscal year 2024, impacted by restructuring expenses and lower sales.
  • Diluted Earnings per Share: $0.68, down from $0.81 in fiscal year 2024, reflecting the decrease in Net Income.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: In fiscal year 2025, automotive accounted for 49% of net sales, medical for 27%, and industrial for 24%.
  • Geographical Performance: The company is expanding its presence in Indiana with a new facility to enhance its medical contract manufacturing organization (CMO) footprint.
  • Sales Units: The company experienced a decrease in sales across all end market verticals in fiscal year 2025, with automotive sales down 11%, medical sales down 7%, and industrial sales down 24%.
  • New Production Launches: Kimball Electronics executed a lease for a new manufacturing facility in Indiana to expand its medical CMO capabilities. This facility will replace the existing Indianapolis facility once fully operational.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates moderate growth in the medical market in fiscal year 2026 and expects consolidated net sales to decline marginally due to the loss of a major automotive program. The company is focused on cost control and strategic investments to strengthen its capabilities and support future growth.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: Kimball Electronics undertook several strategic initiatives to align its cost structure with reduced market demand, including the divestiture of its GES business and the closure of its Tampa facility. The company also focused on expanding its manufacturing capabilities by entering into a lease for a new facility in Indiana to enhance its medical CMO footprint.
  • Capital Management: The company reduced its outstanding debt significantly, with net payments on credit facilities amounting to $147.3 million. It also repurchased $11.958 million of its common stock under the Repurchase Plan, which allows for up to $120 million in buybacks. The company maintained a strong balance sheet with a current ratio of 2.2 and a debt-to-equity ratio of 0.3 as of June 30, 2025.
  • Future Outlook: Kimball Electronics plans to continue its restructuring efforts, including the sale of the Tampa facility, with expected proceeds exceeding restructuring costs. The company anticipates making capital investments for capacity expansions and potential acquisitions to support growth. It also aims to maintain a strong liquidity position, with $88.8 million in cash and $284.7 million in unused credit facilities, to meet its operational needs over the next twelve months.

Challenges and Risks

  • Business and Operational Risks: The loss of key customers or reduction in their purchases could materially affect revenues and profitability. The company is also exposed to risks from customer consolidation, which could lead to increased pricing pressures and dependence on fewer customers.
  • Supply Chain Disruptions: Global supply chain disruptions, particularly in semiconductors, could increase inventory costs and interrupt operations. Inflation and rising supplier prices may impact margins if not passed on to customers.
  • International Operations: The company's substantial international operations expose it to risks such as economic and political instability, foreign currency fluctuations, and compliance with international laws and regulations.
  • Regulatory and Litigation Risks: Compliance with environmental regulations and potential changes in international trade policies could increase operational costs. The company also faces risks related to intellectual property protection and potential litigation.
  • Climate Change: Legal and regulatory initiatives related to climate change could impose significant environmental liabilities and compliance costs.
  • Financial Risks: The company is exposed to credit risks from customers, potential asset impairments, and fluctuations in effective tax rates. Foreign currency risk and interest rate volatility could also impact financial results.
  • Market Conditions: A 13% decrease in net sales in fiscal year 2025 compared to 2024, driven by the loss of a major automotive program and reduced demand in the medical market due to customer overstocking.
  • Cost Control: The company is focusing on cost control while managing future growth prospects, including investments in new facilities and capacity expansions.
  • Customer Retention: The success of the business depends on replacing expiring customers/programs with new ones. The company tracks customer loyalty and new business growth to monitor success in this area.
  • Market Risks: Kimball Electronics is exposed to market risks, including foreign currency risk, interest rate risk, and inflation risk. Fluctuations in exchange rates, rising interest rates, and high levels of inflation could impact operating results and borrowing costs.

SEC Filing: Kimball Electronics, Inc. [ KE ] - 10-K - Aug. 22, 2025

data.title: Spirit Airlines Engages Advisers to Explore Repeat Restructuring — WSJ
data.description:
By Alison Sider and Alexander Gladstone

Spirit Airlines is exploring strategic alternatives after its recent financial restructuring failed to put the budget carrier on a sustainable path, people familiar with the matter said.

The budget airline has brought on financial adviser PJT Partners as it navigates a cash crunch that it previously said raises doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern. It is also working with consulting firms FTI and Seabury Airline Strategy Group.

Spirit is once again on unsteady financial footing. It emerged from bankruptcy in March having eliminated $795 million in debt in a deal with bondholders, who agreed to receive equity in exchange. The bankruptcy process didn't sufficiently resolve problems such as high lease expenses.

Spirit on Thursday announced moves to shore up its position. It borrowed the full $275 million available under its revolver. It also extended a credit-card processing agreement for two years in a deal that allows U.S. Bank National Association to hold back up to $3 million a day.

The airline has said it is exploring ways to bring in cash such as selling planes, real estate or excess gate capacity. It also has new leaders in place who have restructuring and turnaround experience, including Chief Executive Dave Davis.

Spirit's emergence from bankruptcy came at a tough time for the airline industry, which has struggled much of this year with tepid domestic travel demand and a glut of seat capacity that weighed on fares. Discounters like Spirit have faced particular challenges, and Spirit has been trying to attract new customers with higher-end offerings.

In court filings last year, the airline had projected a profit of $252 million for 2025, but it has lost $256 from mid-March through the end of June.

The airline was overly optimistic on its strategic turnaround coming out of bankruptcy and ran into a more difficult environment, said Fitch Ratings analyst Joe Rohlena. Fitch downgraded Spirit last week and said the carrier is "increasingly vulnerable to a default scenario in the near term."

Moody's Ratings, which downgraded Spirit this week, forecast the airline will burn through more than $500 million in cash this year, violating minimum liquidity covenants by year-end.

Write to Alison Sider at alison.sider@wsj.com and Alexander Gladstone at alexander.gladstone@wsj.com

data.title: Tech Up, but Not by Much, on Rate Rotation — Tech Roundup
data.description:
Shares of technology companies rose, but not by as much as the broad market, as traders rotated into more rate-sensitive sectors.

Concerns about valuations in the artificial-intelligence niche lingered even on Friday's bullish session. One strategist said the broad market was priced for perfection, given the combination of record highs and elevated valuations.

"Valuations...are a poor predictor of short-term performance, but the margin for error here is small — that's where that discomfort comes in," said Michael Arone, chief investment strategist at the U.S. SPDR Unit of money manager State Street Global Advisors.

President Trump said the government is taking a nearly 10% stake in chip maker Intel, capping a two-week frenzy in Washington over the future of the company. Shares of Intel added to their recent gains.

Generative AI powerhouse OpenAI is planning to open its first office in India this year, part of its push to meet growing demand for AI in one of its biggest markets.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Northrim BanCorp Approves 4-for-1 Stock Split, Huston Is Named New Chairman
data.description:
By Dean Seal

The holding company for Northrim Bank has approved a four-for-one stock split and tapped its chief executive to succeed its retiring chairman.

Northrim BanCorp said Friday that Joe Schierhorn has disclosed his intent to retire as chairman at the end of this year. CEO Mike Huston will assume the chairmanship on Jan. 1.

Huston has been with the company since 2017 and CEO since last year.

Northrim separately said Friday that the board has authorized a stock split as part of an ongoing review of optimal trading and spread levels. Shares issued in the split will be due after the market closes on Sept. 22 for shareholders of record as of Sept. 18.

Write to Dean Seal at dean.seal@wsj.com

data.title: Modivcare Receives Nasdaq Delisting Notice Following Chapter 11 Filing >MODV
Energy Surges After Powell Speech, Ukraine Doubts — Energy Roundup
data.description:
Shares of energy companies rallied after Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell laid the groundwork for a potential rate cut in September during his speech from a Wyoming symposium.

Oil futures rose as the chances of a cease-fire between Russia and Ukraine faded.

Cenovus Energy is buying rival MEG Energy for about $5.68 billion in the latest consolidation in the Canadian oil-sands region.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com
